s3.label: to redesign one process with AI embedded

pattern.title: Why AI adoption stalls after the rollout.
pattern.body: Most organisations respond to low AI tool usage with a second training session. Same result. The problem is not the tool and it is not the team. It is the process, which was never redesigned to use AI.

rows.text: Enthusiastic early adopters use the tool daily. Visible momentum.
Middle majority try it once or twice on familiar tasks. Irregular usage.
Usage plateaus. Most employees return to previous workflows. Dashboard shows "active licences." Processes have not changed.

premise.attribution: The premise behind every Unlearning School engagement
premise.quote: Adding a tool to an unchanged process does not change the process. You need to redesign the workflow first, and clear the old habit before the new one can form. That is the step most deployments skip.

items.situation: Copilot deployed. Licence spend is real. Process times have not improved. Board is asking for results.
items.result: One process rebuilt in one day. Measurable output. Template your team built themselves.

items.situation: You need a credible AI upskilling programme. EU AI Act Article 4 is in force. No plan that demonstrably changes behaviour.
items.result: Role-adapted workshops. Participants leave with 3–5 AI workflows ready the next morning.

items.situation: Tools are deployed and licensed. Adoption metrics are flat. Business teams blame IT for tools they never asked for.
items.result: A cross-functional adoption layer on top of your stack. Internal champions, governance, and EU AI Act audit trail.

items.title: AI for Non-Technical People
items.body: Structured adoption program for non-technical business teams. Modular: Discovery (1×2h), Short Sprint (2–3 sessions), or Full Program (4+ sessions). Built on the AI tools you already pay for.

items.title: Events & Keynotes
items.body: Workshops, hackathons, and keynotes for fast proof and internal momentum. 90 min – 2 days. 15 to 500 people. Every demo built around your workflows, not generic productivity examples.

items.title: Funding Structuring
items.body: Vendor MDF, AWS partner co-sell, or EU digital grants. We structure the program to qualify, draft the application, and align delivery so reimbursement actually happens.

mechanism.title: Unfreeze,
mechanism.body: Before a team can build new working habits, they must consciously let go of the old ones. Most AI adoption programmes start at "change" and skip "unfreeze" entirely. That is why behaviour does not stick. Our sprints are built around the unfreeze step first.
mechanism.footnote: Based on Lewin's Unfreeze–Change–Refreeze model (1947), applied to AI workflow adoption in non-technical teams.

items.title: 1,400+ professionals trained
items.body: Across finance, retail, manufacturing, and professional services, Romania and EU.

faq.question: What does The Unlearning School do?
faq.answer: We help organisations turn deployed AI tools (Copilot, ChatGPT, Gemini) into actually-used workflows by redesigning processes and training non-technical teams. The focus is behaviour change, not tool demos.

faq.question: Who is this for?
faq.answer: COOs, HR/L&D leads, and CIOs in EU companies that already pay for AI licences but see flat adoption metrics and unchanged process times.

faq.question: Does this help with EU AI Act Article 4 compliance?
faq.answer: Yes. Our role-adapted programmes provide the AI literacy training required by EU AI Act Article 4, with auditable participation and behaviour-change outcomes.

faq.question: How do I start?
faq.answer: Book a free AI Adoption Gap Assessment. One working session, written report within 5 business days, no proposal attached.
